data.title: Is Rayonier (RYN) Being Priced Fairly After Recent Timber REIT Sentiment Shifts
data.description:
• Wondering whether Rayonier's current share price reflects its true value, or if the market is mispricing this timber and land-focused REIT? This article sets out the key facts so you can judge for yourself.
  • Rayonier's shares last closed at US$21.20, with a 2.0% return over the past week and 5.1% over the past month, while year to date the stock shows a 1.9% decline and a 5.6% decline over the past year.
  • Recent news coverage has mainly centered on Rayonier's positioning within the Specialized REITs space and how timber and land assets fit into diversified income portfolios. Commentary has also highlighted how sentiment toward interest rate expectations and real assets has fed into recent price moves.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Rayonier scores 3 out of 6. This raises the question of what different valuation approaches are signaling today, and whether there is an even richer way to think about value that will be covered at the end of this article.

Find out why Rayonier's -5.6% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Rayonier Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting future funds from operations and then discounting those cash flows back to today’s value.

For Rayonier, the model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on funds from operations. The latest twelve months free cash flow is given as $652 million. Looking ahead, analysts provide explicit forecasts up to 2028, with free cash flow for that year projected at $497.8 million. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates further, with ten year projections that rise from $370.1 million in 2026 to $786.8 million in 2035, with each year discounted back to a present value.

Pulling all of these discounted cash flows together, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $44.47 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $21.20, this implies Rayonier is trading at a 52.3% discount to the DCF estimate, which suggests the shares may be materially undervalued on this model alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Rayonier is undervalued by 52.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 57 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Rayonier.

Approach 2: Rayonier Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay per share to the earnings that support that share price. Higher growth expectations and lower perceived risk usually justify a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher risk usually point to a lower, more conservative P/E being seen as fair.

Rayonier currently trades on a P/E of 94.12x. That compares with an average P/E of 16.22x for the Specialized REITs industry and a peer average of 44.46x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” for Rayonier of 54.05x, which is the P/E level suggested after factoring in elements such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the broad industry because it adjusts for Rayonier’s own characteristics rather than assuming all REITs or all peers deserve similar multiples. Against this yardstick, Rayonier’s actual P/E of 94.12x sits well above the Fair Ratio of 54.05x. On this measure, it reflects a relatively rich earnings multiple.

Result: OVERVALUED

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Rayonier Narrative

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation. This is where Narratives come in as your way to give a clear story behind the numbers for Rayonier, tying your view on its future revenue, earnings and margins to a Fair Value that can be compared with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an accessible tool used by millions of investors, helping you link a company’s story to a financial forecast and then to a Fair Value that updates automatically when fresh information such as news or earnings is added.

With Rayonier, for example, one Narrative might lean toward the higher Fair Value of about US$32.99, based on expectations for stronger earnings and a future P/E of roughly 27x. Another might sit closer to the lower Fair Value of about US$24 to US$27, assuming more modest revenues and an earnings profile that justifies a different P/E. Comparing any of these Fair Values to the current price can help you decide whether the stock looks closer to fully priced or on sale according to your own story.

data.description:

Revenue estimates were cut sharply as the analysts signalled a weaker outlook - perhaps a sign that investors should temper their expectations as well.

If met, this would reflect a major 63% improvement in sales compared to the last 12 months. Before the latest update, the analysts were foreseeing CA$58b of revenue in 2026. It looks like forecasts have become a fair bit less optimistic on Manulife Financial, given the substantial drop in revenue estimates.

Check out our latest analysis for Manulife Financial

There was no particular change to the consensus price target of CA$54.78, with Manulife Financial's latest outlook seemingly not enough to result in a change of valuation.

Taking a look at the bigger picture now, one of the ways we can understand these forecasts is to see how they compare to both past performance and industry growth estimates. For example, we noticed that Manulife Financial's rate of growth is expected to accelerate meaningfully, with revenues forecast to exhibit 63% growth to the end of 2026 on an annualised basis. That is well above its historical decline of 20% a year over the past five years. By contrast, our data suggests that other companies (with analyst coverage) in the industry are forecast to see their revenue grow 6.8% per year. So it looks like Manulife Financial is expected to grow faster than its competitors, at least for a while.

data.title: Nerds on Site Insiders Still CA$33k Away From Original Investment Value
data.description:
Insiders who bought CA$111.3k worth of Nerds on Site Inc. (CSE:NERD) stock in the last year recovered part of their losses as the stock rose by 20% last week. However, the purchase is proving to be a costly gamble, since losses made by insiders have totalled CA$33k since the time of purchase.

While we would never suggest that investors should base their decisions solely on what the directors of a company have been doing, we do think it is perfectly logical to keep tabs on what insiders are doing.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

The Last 12 Months Of Insider Transactions At Nerds on Site

Over the last year, we can see that the biggest insider purchase was by CEO & Director Charles Regan for CA$100k worth of shares, at about CA$0.045 per share. So it's clear an insider wanted to buy, even at a higher price than the current share price (being CA$0.03). While their view may have changed since the purchase was made, this does at least suggest they have had confidence in the company's future. In our view, the price an insider pays for shares is very important. It is encouraging to see an insider paid above the current price for shares, as it suggests they saw value, even at higher levels. The only individual insider to buy over the last year was Charles Regan.

Charles Regan purchased 2.60m shares over the year. The average price per share was CA$0.043. The chart below shows insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year. If you want to know exactly who sold, for how much, and when, simply click on the graph below!

View our latest analysis for Nerds on Site

Nerds on Site is not the only stock insiders are buying. So take a peek at this free list of under-the-radar companies with insider buying.

Does Nerds on Site Boast High Insider Ownership?

Looking at the total insider shareholdings in a company can help to inform your view of whether they are well aligned with common shareholders. A high insider ownership often makes company leadership more mindful of shareholder interests. Insiders own 9.7% of Nerds on Site shares, worth about CA$267k, according to our data. However, it's possible that insiders might have an indirect interest through a more complex structure. Overall, this level of ownership isn't that impressive, but it's certainly better than nothing!

So What Do The Nerds on Site Insider Transactions Indicate?

Insider purchases may have been minimal, in the last three months, but there was no selling at all. The net investment is not enough to encourage us much. On a brighter note, the transactions over the last year are encouraging. The transactions are fine but it'd be more encouraging if Nerds on Site insiders bought more shares in the company. So while it's helpful to know what insiders are doing in terms of buying or selling, it's also helpful to know the risks that a particular company is facing.
